mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz CLA 220d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned CLA 220d engine typically costs £2,000–£7,500 supply only depending on generation. OM651-era engines (C117) sit at the lower end (£2,000–£5,500), while OM654 units (C118/X118) run £2,500–£7,500. A full engine replacement can run between £1,500 and £5,000 on average, with Mercedes engines typically at the higher end of the range. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on workshop location. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz CLA 220d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine generation your CLA has. The OM651 (C117, 2013–2019) is fundamentally reliable — 200,000–300,000 km examples are common — but has predictable weak points: timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km is the single most critical failure point. Early versions had problems that were resolved in later versions with improved components such as chain tensioners. The OM654 (C118/X118, 2019–present) is considered more reliable than the OM651, but injector seals and timing chain wear still need attention. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz CLA 220d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on the OM651 (C117) — the most critical failure; early Delphi piezo injector failure on OM651 units; EGR valve and cooler clogging on both OM651 and OM654; DPF clogging and sudden drops in fuel economy; and water pump leaks. The OM651 timing chain is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement labour-intensive.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz CLA 220d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold: if your CLA is worth £8,000–£15,000 and a recon engine is £4,000 (27–50%), it's worth it. If the vehicle is a tired £6,000 example, the same engine exceeds 50% — get a scrap valuation first. For OM654-engined cars (C118/X118, 2019+), higher vehicle values make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz CLA 220d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: C117 (2013–2019) is OM651 (2.1L, 170 PS); C118/X118 (2019–present) is OM654 (2.0L, 190 PS). The CLA 220d replaced the earlier CLA 220 CDI model. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM651 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic failure requiring a full engine replacement (£2,000–£5,500+). Early warning signs of timing chain noise should prompt immediate action. The chain is located at the rear of the engine, making this a labour-intensive job. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil and filter changes with good quality oil of the recommended viscosity should ensure a long life.

eraMap.title: The 220d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: 2.1L four-cylinder diesel — timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km; injector and EGR issues
2.0L four-cylinder diesel — more refined and efficient, but injector seals and timing chain need attention

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates. A full engine replacement in the UK can run between £1,500 and £5,000 on average, with Mercedes engines typically at the higher end of the range. Reconditioned engines are available with 12-month warranties from UK suppliers. Older OM651 units generally sit at the lower end of the range; OM654 units command a premium.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLA 220d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The transverse engine layout in the CLA requires more labour than longitudinal installations in rear-wheel-drive Mercedes models.

cta.label: → Compare CLA 220d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (OM651 — C117 2013–2019)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or slapping noise from the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light, poor performance, rough idling. Early versions of the OM651 appear to have had problems that were resolved in later versions with improved components such as chain tensioners. A stretched timing chain can jump teeth, causing severe engine damage including bent valves and piston damage. The tensioner is oil-fed, so inadequate oil volume or pressure can accelerate wear. The timing chain is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement labour-intensive.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; if the chain jumps, it's an interference engine — catastrophic failure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The OM651's timing chain is its single most critical failure point. Early warning signs of timing chain noise should prompt immediate action. Address rattling noises immediately to prevent severe engine damage. Regular oil and filter changes with good quality oil of the recommended viscosity should ensure a long life. At the first sign of rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately — preventative chain replacement is far cheaper than a full engine rebuild.

problems.title: EGR Valve & Cooler Clogging (OM651 & OM654)
problems.symptoms: Reduced performance, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, limp mode, hesitation under load. EGR and cooler systems can become clogged with exhaust soot and grime. Some owners report sudden drops in fuel economy from 60+ MPG down to 30 MPG, which can indicate DPF regeneration or EGR issues. A failed EGR valve can cause rough running and stalling.
problems.typicalMileage: 50,000–100,000 miles — accelerated by short urban journeys.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, performance will continue to degrade.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: Both the OM651 and OM654 are sensitive to EGR clogging, particularly with short urban journeys. The ECU compares requested vs measured air mass — when airflow becomes inconsistent, the car can enter limp mode. Regular motorway runs can help keep the EGR system clean. If you experience a sudden drop in fuel economy or hesitation, have the EGR system inspected promptly.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Failure (OM651)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfires, decreased performance, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, fuel smell in the oil. The OM651's early Delphi piezo injectors are the most common failure point, causing erratic fuel delivery.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, misfires can damage catalytic converters.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: Early Delphi piezo injectors fitted to OM651 engines are sensitive to fuel quality and operating conditions. They can cause erratic fuel delivery, rough running, and higher consumption. If you experience rough idle or misfires, have the injectors inspected. Later OM651 engines received more durable magnetic solenoid injectors.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Clean or replace — do it when warnings appear
Replace affected injectors — upgrade to magnetic solenoid type
Forced regeneration or clean — do it when warning appears
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLA 220d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2019 OM654 (C118/X118) with the updated engine design — more refined and reliable
* OM651 (C117) with full service history and regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* OM651 with the improved timing chain tensioner components
* Full service history with regular DPF-friendly driving (motorway miles)
avoidIf: * OM651 with cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch is imminent
* OM651 with rough idle or misfires — early injector failure
* No service history on any generation — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* EGR or DPF warning lights — evidence of clogging issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The CLA 220d badge spans two generations of Mercedes diesel. The OM651 (C117, 2013–2019) is fundamentally reliable but has predictable weak points — timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km is the single most critical issue. The OM654 (C118/X118, 2019–present) is a more refined, lighter and generally more reliable engine. With the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold, a £4,000–£5,000 reconditioned engine on a CLA valued at £8,000–£15,000 is 27–50% of the car's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. On a sub-£6,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare CLA 220d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz CLA 220d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (OM651 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (OM651/OM654) and oil-interval discipline
* Injector History — For OM651, check if injectors have been replaced
* DPF Status — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys accelerate clogging
* EGR System — Any hesitation or sudden drop in fuel economy? (EGR clogging)
* Smoke Check — Black smoke could indicate injector or DPF issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
